description: description
responsibilities: Specialist - Governance, Risk and Compliance As a Delivery Excellence Specialist at ZS Associates, you will be responsible for driving delivery excellence by ensuring that delivery processes are executed with precision, consistency, and alignment to organizational standards. You will provide strategic and technical guidance to delivery teams, enabling them to implement and sustain high-performing controls and practices that support delivery efficiency and continuous improvement. By collaborating with key stakeholders—including Delivery Managers and Leaders - you will help deliver a comprehensive view of delivery health, performance metrics, and their impact on business outcomes. Your advanced knowledge of delivery management principles and project execution will empower you to lead innovative initiatives, optimize delivery frameworks, and manage a diverse team in a dynamic and evolving technology landscape. What You'll Do: Specialist - Governance, Risk and Compliance in the Enterprise will… Work closely with delivery teams to embed best practices in delivery excellence, risk management, ensuring successful outcomes for aligned programs Track and report operational KPIs to support data-driven decision-making. Monitor delivery performance metrics and escalate issues to ensure timely resolution Collaborate with cross-functional teams to drive continuous improvement in delivery practices. Conduct process reviews and identify inefficiencies, recommending actionable improvements. Assist in designing and implementing process improvement initiatives using Lean, Six Sigma, or Agile methodologies. Identify operational bottlenecks and support initiatives to enhance efficiency and effectiveness. Participate in change management initiatives by supporting communication, training, and stakeholder engagement activities. Help assess the impact of changes and gather feedback to refine implementation strategies. Promote adoption of new tools, processes, and systems across teams. Perform periodic project audits / reviews for the project delivery process based on CMMI (Capability Maturity Model Integration) process areas and report non compliances, best practices followed Assist in Delivery Risk governance and work closely with QRM Leads ensuring effective risk management and mitigation throughout lifecycle of projects Conduct trainings on Delivery Excellence focus areas, QMS process assets and awareness on best practices Maintain and ensure project process maturity (CMMI Level 3) adherence for ensuring Capability Maturity Model Integration (CMMI) v3.0 Development & Services appraisal and accreditation process Showcase expertise on Software Quality Management framework such as ISO9001, CMMI Development, CMMI Services and focus on performance and continuous improvement at project as well as organization level Enhance Delivery and Operations maturity through close engagement and planned intervention across all project phases Definition and regular enhancement of enterprise level Quality Management System aka Enterprise Delivery Framework (EDF) supporting Development (Waterfall, Agile) as well as Operations projects (Services delivery methodology. What You'll Bring: Bachelor's degree in IT or relevant field with a strong academic record. A minimum of 7-10 years of experience in Delivery Excellence and Quality Management Strong communication & strategic influencing skills. Relevant experience working with senior leaders, building internal networks, and delivering high impact programs in complex -matrixed environments. Formal training or certification in Delivery/Operations excellence, and/or 5+ years of experience or equivalent expertise in technology Delivery excellence management, Operations excellence, Software Quality Management & Software Quality Standards or related field. Familiarity with delivery excellence frameworks, industry standards, and pharma regulatory requirements (HIPPA, GXP etc) Proficient knowledge and expertise in data security, risk assessment & reporting, control evaluation, design, and governance, with a proven record of implementing effective risk mitigation strategies. Proficient in MS Office productivity suite (e.g., Word, Excel, PowerPoint, SharePoint). Advanced Excel skills strongly preferred Six Sigma Black Belt/ITIL/CMMI/ISO9001 Lead auditor/MBA or relevant Delivery excellence / Audit certification Experience with Project management tools (e.g., Atlassian JIRA, Confluence, MS Project) and Quality Assurance tools CMMI implementation experience (Maturity level 3 at minimum/ High maturity implementation experience preferred ) Willingness to adapt to evolving industry standards and technologies Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approac

description: description
responsibilities: The Senior Governance & Risk Administrator will serve as a key member of our IT Governance, Risk, and Compliance team, responsible for proactively identifying and mitigating risks, ensuring compliance with regulations, and enhancing our control framework. The role involves working closely with various stakeholders, analyzing security findings, and providing input into the development and maintenance of security risk scorecards. The candidate will also assist in the management of GRC tools and contribute to various risk governance-related initiatives and special projects. What you'll do: Conduct Risk Assessments: Collaborate with the ZS stakeholders from IT, HR, Finance, Legal, etc. teams to perform risk assessments and identify potential threats and vulnerabilities in our IT infrastructure and third-party relationships. Third Party Risk Management (TPRM): Develop, implement, and maintain effective TPRM processes, including third-party risk assessment, due diligence, and ongoing monitoring. Remediation Oversight: Assist in the management of remediation activities, including the development and monitoring of remediation plans for identified risks and vulnerabilities. Documentation and Reporting: Prepare comprehensive findings reports for various stakeholders, summarizing assessment results, remediation progress, and recommended actions, both internally and within the TPRM framework. Audit Support: Provide support during internal and external audits, assisting in audit planning, execution, communication, and reporting phases, with a specific focus on TPRM. Security Monitoring: Analyze findings from security monitoring systems, reviewing vulnerabilities for active and acceptable remediation plans, including third-party risks. Risk Mitigation: Collaborate with cross-functional teams to identify and proactively address potential gaps in security, especially in the context of third-party risks. GRC Tools: Assist in the management and maintenance of GRC tools, including configuration and reporting, with a focus on TPRM capabilities. Policy and Framework Compliance: Ensure that operational controls, including those related to third parties, are aligned with relevant control frameworks, standards, and regulatory requirements. Training and Awareness: Contribute to the development of information security training material and assist in conducting training sessions for relevant stakeholders, emphasizing TPRM best practices. Special Projects: Collaborate on various technology risk governance initiatives and other special projects as assigned, with a strong emphasis on TPRM improvements. Mentorship: Lead and mentor a team of Governance & Risk Analysts/Administrators to ensure efficient execution of risk assessment processes, risk treatment activities, and third-party risk management. What you'll bring: Bachelor's degree in IT or relevant field with a strong academic record. A minimum of 4 years of experience in IT Risk Management and Third-Party Risk Management roles. Knowledge and experience in conducting risk assessments, managing remediation activities, and enhancing TPRM practices. Familiarity with Industry standards and frameworks like ISO 27001, ISO 27701, ISO 27017, ISO 27018, NIST CSF, etc. Strong communication skills, both written and verbal, for reporting and interacting with stakeholders. Knowledge of control frameworks, information security policies, regulatory compliance, and TPRM best practices. Ability to work independently and as part of a team. Willingness to adapt to evolving industry standards and technologies. Certifications such as CISA, CISSP, or other relevant GRC and TPRM certifications are a plus. Proficiency in MS Office, including Word, Excel, and PowerPoint. Experience with GRC tools (e.g., RSA Archer) and software for reporting and compliance management, with a focus on TPRM capabilities. Basic understanding of web-based applications, operating systems, databases, and TPRM tools. Knowledge of laws and regulations impacting data security, privacy, and third-party risk management is a plus

description: description
responsibilities: ZS IT Support teams are aligned with the company’s business strategy and operating model and aims to provide its 10,000 plus employees and their clients the right tools and information for high performance. The IT organization focuses on providing products and services to ZS to ensure successful business outcomes. This involves providing a scalable, sustainable and reliable IT infrastructure, customized applications, messaging and collaboration products, Business Intelligence and Database administration support along with a reliable 24*7 uninterrupted high-quality technology support services.   TECHNICAL SUPPORT ASSOCIATE Technical Support Associates support the work activities of the Network Administrator, maintaining a user environment consisting of laptop and desktop computers running the standard suite of business applications. What You’ll Do: Technical Support Associate in the Enterprise will… Configure computer hardware and software, as well as troubleshoot related problems.  Complete some administrative tasks, such as initiating hardware repairs, assisting with organizational efforts and asset management. IT team projects as assigned. Knowledge and experience with PC desktop support functions relative to laptop and desktop computers. Installation and configuration of business application software (i.e., MS Windows, Mac OS, Office 365 Suite including SharePoint and MS Teams.). Technical Support tools: Norton Ghost, VNC. Proficiency in PC hardware configuration, troubleshooting and maintenance. Experience with Microsoft Active Directory, Wireless Networking (client side), Collaboration tools like Zoom and MS teams. Fair knowledge on troubleshooting zoom rooms and infrastructure related to it. Experience in troubleshooting various forms of software problems (e.g., operating system and application programs). What You’ll Bring: BS/BE with record of high academic achievement   A+ certification or Microsoft Certified Systems Administrator. Eagerness to contribute in a team-oriented environment. Ability to work methodically and analytically in a quantitative problem-solving environment. Excellent leadership, communication and organizational skills Strong customer service skills Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approac

description: description
responsibilities: Human Resources develops and executes successful recruiting programs, learning and expertise-development initiatives, strategic staffing assignments, effective reporting and analytics, performance management processes, and work-life benefits and operations. HR Associate - Benefits What you’ll do: Execute day-to-day benefits administration tasks, including employee data changes, enrollments, eligibility updates, and life event processing, in accordance with established procedures Support ongoing benefits operations by handling high-volume, transactional activities efficiently and accurately Perform routine audits and reconciliations to ensure data integrity between Benefits systems, Payroll, and HR systems Support efforts to standardize, streamline, and document global benefits processes Assist with the transition of manual or repetitive tasks into automated or semi-automated workflows Identify process gaps, inefficiencies, or recurring issues and escalate recommendations for improvement Partner closely with the Payroll team to support benefits-related payroll inputs, deductions, and issue resolution Coordinate with the India-based Benefits team member to ensure smooth handoffs and clear ownership of tasks Respond to internal inquiries related to benefits operations, routing complex questions to senior team members as appropriate Maintain process documentation, job aids, and standard operating procedures (SOPs) Track and report on operational metrics such as turnaround times, error rates, and volume trends Ensure adherence to internal controls, compliance requirements, and data privacy standards
qualifications: What you’ll bring: Bachelor’s degree or equivalent experience 1–3 years of experience in HR Operations, Benefits Administration, Payroll, or an HR shared services environment Strong attention to detail and ability to manage high-volume of work Comfort working with HRIS, payroll systems, and Excel or similar tools Ability to follow standardized processes while identifying opportunities for improvement Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approach

description: description
responsibilities: What You’ll Do   Manage day-to-day operations of AWS infrastructure across multiple accounts and environments (Prod, Stage, Dev). Handle incident management, troubleshooting, and root cause analysis for AWS services such as EC2, S3, RDS, Lambda, EMR, CloudWatch, and IAM. Ensure high availability, reliability, and performance of cloud-based applications. Monitor system health, utilization, and cost metrics using AWS-native tools (CloudWatch, Cost Explorer, etc.). Perform regular operational activities including access management, resource optimization, patching coordination, and service requests. Collaborate with Engineering, Security, and Data teams to enable secure and scalable cloud architectures. Support infrastructure provisioning and management using Terraform modules and templates. Assist in maintaining consistency between desired and actual cloud states (drift detection and remediation) Contribute to automation of repetitive operational tasks to improve efficiency and reduce manual effort. Work with CI/CD pipelines to enable infrastructure deployment and updates. Strong ownership mindset and accountability for platform stability. Proactive communication and stakeholder management. Ability to work collaboratively across teams. Continuous learning mindset with focus on cloud advancements. What You’ll Bring   BS/BE with record of high academic achievement   Bachelor's Degree in CS, IT or EE with record of high academic achievement. 2+ years of experience in AWS and on-premise infrastructure security administration. Experience on participating MLPS and understanding of related controls is a plus. Possess strong verbal, written and team presentation communication skills. Ability to work within a global (US and India) team environment and contribute to the overall timely delivery of multiple projects. Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approac

description: description
responsibilities: What you'll do: Hardware Lifecycle Management Manage the complete hardware lifecycle — from procurement, deployment, and tracking to maintenance, refresh, and retirement/disposal. Maintain accurate records of all hardware assets including laptops, desktops, servers, peripherals, and network equipment. Define and enforce hardware refresh cycles in alignment with ZS's technology standards and budget cycles. Coordinate with IT procurement and finance for hardware sourcing, vendor evaluation, and purchase approvals. Ensure proper decommissioning and environmentally compliant disposal (ITAD) of end-of-life assets. Software Lifecycle Management Oversee the full lifecycle of software assets including procurement, deployment, utilization tracking, compliance, and retirement. Conduct regular software license audits and reconciliation to ensure compliance with vendor agreements. Identify underutilized licenses and optimize the software portfolio to reduce waste and cost. Manage software packaging, deployment workflows, and version upgrade planning in coordination with IT operations teams. SaaS & End-User Software Management Serve as the central point of contact for end-user software and SaaS platform needs across the organization. Evaluate and onboard new SaaS tools by assessing business need, security posture, and licensing terms. Manage SaaS subscriptions, user provisioning/deprovisioning, and utilization reporting. Partner with business stakeholders to rationalize the SaaS portfolio and eliminate redundant tools. Maintain a healthy pool of pre-purchased licenses for frequently requested software to enable rapid provisioning. Set and manage minimum license stock thresholds to prevent fulfillment delays. Forecast license demand based on headcount trends, project pipelines, and historical usage patterns. Renewals Management Own the end-to-end renewals calendar for all hardware maintenance contracts and software/SaaS subscriptions. Proactively engage internal stakeholders and vendors well in advance of renewal dates to avoid lapses in coverage. Evaluate renewal terms, negotiate pricing, and recommend consolidation or discontinuation where appropriate. Maintain a renewal tracking system with automated alerts and approval workflows. Chargeback Management Design and administer an IT chargeback / show back model to allocate hardware and software costs accurately to business units. Work with Finance and IT leadership to define chargeback policies, cost allocation logic, and reporting cadence. Provide monthly/quarterly chargeback reports and resolve billing queries from internal teams. Asset Inventory & CMDB Accuracy Maintain a single, authoritative, and continuously updated asset inventory using an ITSM/ITAM tool (e.g., ServiceNow, Snow Software, Flexera). Conduct periodic physical and virtual audits to validate inventory accuracy. Integrate asset data with the CMDB (Configuration Management Database) to support incident, change, and problem management processes. Define and enforce asset tagging, naming conventions, and classification standards. Governance & Compliance Establish and institutionalize an IT Asset Management (ITAM) governance framework aligned with industry standards (ISO 19770, ITIL). Define policies, processes, and controls for asset request, approval, issuance, transfer, and return. Ensure compliance with software licensing agreements, audit readiness, and vendor contractual obligations. Prepare and present asset management KPIs, dashboards, and reports to IT leadership. Team Management Lead, mentor, and develop a team of IT asset analysts/coordinators. Set performance goals, conduct regular reviews, and foster a culture of accountability and continuous improvement. Delegate responsibilities effectively and build team capability in ITAM tools and processes. Stakeholder Collaboration Act as a trusted advisor to IT, Finance, Legal, Procurement, and business unit leaders on all asset-related matters. Drive cross-functional initiatives to improve asset utilization, reduce costs, and strengthen governance. Technical Knowledge Strong understanding of software licensing models — perpetual, subscription, concurrent, named-user, OEM, and enterprise agreements (Microsoft EA, Adobe VIP, etc.). Working knowledge of CMDB (Configuration Management Database) and its integration with ITAM tools. Familiarity with IT security principles as they relate to asset tracking, data wiping, and secure disposal (ITAD). Understanding of networking fundamentals — enough to track and manage network-attached hardware assets. Proficiency in data analysis tools — advanced Excel is a must; Power BI or Tableau is a strong plus. Certifications Candidates are expected to hold at least one core IAITAM certification, with the preferred progression being CSAM (software licensing & compliance) and CHAMP (hardware lifecycle management) leading up to the flagship CITAM (Certified IT Asset Manager) credential. Complementary certifications in ITIL v4 Foundation, CAMSE (ITAM + cybersecurity), and familiarity with ISO/IEC 19770-1 standards are strongly preferred Other Preferred Experience Exposure to FinOps principles and cloud asset management (AWS, Azure, GCP). Experience in professional services, consulting, or technology company environment. Experience with SOX, GDPR, or similar compliance audits as they relate to IT assets. Knowledge of responsible IT Asset Disposal (ITAD) practices and environmental regulations (e-waste). What you'll bring: Education Bachelor’s degree in information technology, Computer Science, Business Administration, or a related field. A master’s degree (MBA or MS in IT) is a plus. Experience 10–12 years of progressive experience in IT Asset Management Minimum 2–3 years of supervisory or team leadership experience. Demonstrated experience managing end-to-end hardware and software lifecycles in a mid-to-large enterprise environment. Hands-on experience with ITAM/ITSM platforms such as ServiceNow SAM, Snow Software, Flexera, Ivanti ITAM Suite, or Lansweeper. Proven experience in SaaS portfolio governance, including vendor onboarding, subscription management, and utilization reviews. Experience designing and administering IT chargeback/show back models in collaboration with Finance. Track record of managing software license compliance and successfully navigating vendor audits (Microsoft, Adobe, Oracle, etc.). Experience with hardware refresh programs and OEM vendor management (Dell, HP, Lenovo, Apple

description: description
responsibilities: Finance Associate - Client Accounting (Sales Operations) We seek a financial professional for a Finance Associate - Client Accounting role in our Pune, India office. As an Associate in Sales Operations / Order Management, within Client Accounting, you will support the contract-to-project setup process for professional services engagements helping ensure signed client documents (e.g., MSA/SOW/PO) are translated into accurate SAP customer project/WBS and billing setup so delivery teams can execute and Finance can invoice and apply revenue policies. Your work is execution-focused and completed within defined SOPs, templates, and checklists, with routine review by senior team members and clear escalation paths for exceptions. What you’ll do: Contract intake, completeness checks, and operational validation (under supervision) Perform initial review for completeness and readiness of incoming client documents (e.g., fully executed agreement, required fields/data present), and organize the contract record so it is accessible to internal stakeholders. Validate that key operational terms are clear enough to enable project setup and billing setup, and flag gaps/ambiguities for senior/Finance/Legal review rather than interpreting them independently. SAP services project setup support (functional end-user scope) You will support foundational setup activities that enable billing, using guided processes rather than configuration work. Support creation/maintenance of billing items for customer projects, in collaboration or overlap with billing team (including selecting contract type such as Fixed Price or Time & Expenses; assigning work packages; entering amounts/caps; maintaining billing due dates/billing plan items; and capturing customer references such as PO) Cross-functional coordination (primarily internal-facing) Coordinate with Sales, Delivery/Project teams, and finance to collect missing inputs, clarify basic items (e.g., dates, PO references, billing schedule), and keep the setup moving through the standard workflow. Provide routine status updates and document actions/decisions taken, maintaining an audit-ready trail of changes and approvals as required by the workflow. Documentation hygiene and operational reporting support Maintain organized records and trackers (e.g., contract intake status, setup progress, exception logs) to support day-to-day visibility and handoffs. Support basic operational reporting aligned to the process (e.g., volumes processed, cycle time, rework themes) as defined by the team’s governance model. Contract elements you will typically review and validate within Associate scope of experience & expertise You will focus on operationally actionable contract elements that drive project setup and billing structure—not legal interpretation. Common validation checklist items include: Scope/deliverables and timelines: ensuring deliverables, milestones, and timelines are stated clearly enough to align to project structure and billing phases. Payment structure and triggers: confirming whether billing is milestone-based, periodic, or time & expenses—and ensuring the setup can reflect those triggers. Basic contract data required for setup: parties/contact information, effective/expiration dates, and required customer references (e.g., PO) for linkage to sales/billing documents. Billing plan and method alignment: verifying that the invoice method and revenue method classification selected/entered aligns to the contracted payment structure (and escalating mismatches). Revenue recognition awareness (coordination-only; no accounting ownership) You are expected to have awareness of ASC 606/IFRS 15 concepts and recognize when contract terms may impact Finance’s revenue decisions; however, you do not determine accounting treatment, standalone selling price allocation, or final revenue recognition conclusions. Practical awareness expected: Familiarity with the five-step model structure (identify contract; identify performance obligations; determine transaction price; allocate price; recognize revenue) at a high level, so you can spot terms that could affect setup and downstream treatment. Accuracy and attention to detail: when translating contract terms into SAP billing/project setup fields. Process discipline: ability to follow SOPs, maintain documentation, and preserve required audit trails. Clear written communication: for internal coordination and structured escalation. Comfort working with global stakeholders: through defined handoffs, including support for contracts across regions via documented workflows.
qualifications: What you’ll bring: Master’s degree in finance, economics, or business required, with a strong record of academic achievement. 0-3 years of Sales and Order Management experience. Strong working knowledge of Microsoft Office (Excel, Word, PPT, Outlook), SAP experience desirable; Strong analytic and critical thinking abilities. Effective oral and written communication skills that enable strong relationship-building and stakeholder management. Strong attention to detail. Motivated and proactive. Professional objectivity and judgment to know when to challenge activities. Ability to multi-task in a fast paced, rapidly-changing environment. Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approach How you’ll grow: Cross-functional skills development & custom learning pathways Milestone training programs aligned to career progression opportunities Internal mobility paths that empower growth via s-curves, individual contribution and role expansions Hybrid working model: ZS is committed to a Flexible and Connected way of working. ZSers are onsite at clients or ZS offices three days a week. Combined flexibility to work remotely two days a week is also available. The magic of ZS culture and innovation thrives in both planned and spontaneous face-to-face connections.

description: description
responsibilities: ZS’s Supply Chain & Manufacturing (SCM) practice is dedicated to helping clients unlock value and achieve operational excellence through digital transformation. With a team of over 200 experts and practitioners, ZS partners with leading organizations worldwide to drive innovation across the manufacturing value chain. The team has successfully delivered more than 100 digital transformation projects globally, spanning industries such as automotive, life sciences, consumer products, and industrials. As a Digital Transformation Leader for Manufacturing at ZS (Technology Associate Principal), you will drive end-to-end digital transformation initiatives for global manufacturing clients. You will leverage deep expertise in Industry 4.0/5.0, IoT, AI, and advanced analytics to architect and execute strategies that deliver measurable business value. You will partner with C-suite executives, lead cross-functional teams, and ensure successful adoption of digital solutions across the manufacturing value chain. This person will need to fully understand the company and its service lines and actively assess/present ways to serve clients by developing solutions and offerings relevant to that client base. The successful candidate will work closely with ZS leadership; grow the practice by expanding client and customer relationships and building trusted advisory relationships with executives at the company’s various accounts.  What you'll do: Develop and execute digital transformation strategies for manufacturing clients, from vision to value realization. Lead large-scale Industry 4.0/5.0 programs, including IoT, IIoT, cloud, AI/ML, and automation projects across sectors such as Automotive, Life Sciences, Oil & Gas, Agriculture, and Consumer Products.Manage end-to-end project delivery, ensuring on-time, within-budget execution and alignment with client objectives. Develop and manage client relationships on projects. Deeply understand client’s business challenges and provide thought leadership in addressing issues. Identify opportunities and contribute to the selling of additional work to new and existing clients; Active involvement in pre-sales opportunities, building new assets and platforms that will support ZS strategic growth Architect and implement digital solution (e.g., digital twins, predictive analytics, smart sensors, process automation) to optimize manufacturing operations, supply chain, quality and maintenance. Have an eye on Industrial/technology disruptions and develop POVs on how to help clients deal with the same. Drive organizational change management, ensuring adoption of new technologies and digital ways of working. Lead workshops, training, and stakehoder engagement to foster a culture of innovation and data-driven decision making. Serve as a trusted advisor to C-level executives, shaping their digital agendas and guiding strategic investments. Represent ZS at industry forums, publish thought leadership, and contribute to the firm’s digital manufacturing practice growth.
qualifications: What you’ll bring: Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, or related field; MBA preferred. 18+ years of professional experience spanning consulting and core industry roles, with a strong blend of strategic advisory, digital transformation leadership, and hands‑on operational exposure in manufacturing environments. Significant tenure in the consulting industry, driving large‑scale transformation programs for global clients, including roadmap definition, operating model design, capability building, and delivery oversight for digital manufacturing, IT‑OT integration, and Industry 4.0/5.0 initiatives. Prior foundational experience in the core manufacturing industry, providing deep understanding of real‑world plant operations, production processes, supply chain dynamics, and technology adoption challenges within complex industrial setups. Deep expertise in Industry 4.0/5.0, IoT/IIoT, cloud platforms, AI/ML, automation, and IT‑OT integration, along with hands‑on experience implementing digital manufacturing solutions (MES, QMS, LIMS, PIMS, SCADA, historians, edge computing, etc.). Strong knowledge of manufacturing processes across multiple sectors (e.g., Automotive, Life Sciences, Oil & Gas, FMCG), combining core industry exposure with consulting‑led transformation experience. Proven ability to lead cross‑functional and multi‑geography teams, manage P&L or portfolio-level financials, and deliver complex, multi‑tower digital programs in advisory and delivery roles. Certifications such as PMP, Scrum Master, SIRI Assessor, or equivalent are a plus. How you’ll grow: Cross-functional skills development & custom learning pathways Milestone training programs aligned to career progression opportunities Internal mobility paths that empower growth via s-curves, individual contribution and role expansions Hybrid working model: ZS is committed to a Flexible and Connected way of working. ZSers are onsite at clients or ZS offices three days a week. Combined flexibility to work remotely two days a week is also available. The magic of ZS culture and innovation thrives in both planned and spontaneous face-to-face connections.

description: description
responsibilities: We are seeking a Visual Designer (Associate) to join our Creative & Communications team. In this role, you will contribute to creating clear, concept-driven visual communication across formats and mediums - translating complex ideas into structured, engaging, and user-centric design solutions. You will work closely with cross-functional teams to understand requirements and develop outputs that align with brand systems, communication goals, and user needs. A strong focus on design craft, attention to detail, and consistency in execution is essential, along with a solid foundation in layout, typography, and information hierarchy. This role offers the opportunity to work across diverse projects while strengthening your approach to visual storytelling, system thinking, and high-quality execution within a collaborative and evolving design environment. What You’ll Do:   Design & Execution Develop visually compelling and conceptually rich communication across formats, including campaigns, visual narratives, and communication assets Create high-quality layouts, graphics, and visual systems for print and digital collaterals Translate complex information into clear, engaging, and well-structured visual outputs Apply strong design craft - typography, color, composition, and attention to detail - to ensure polished and consistent work. Collaboration & Delivery Work closely with internal, business and project teams to align on objectives, messaging, and visual direction Work on multiple projects with a focus on quality, timelines, and consistency Contribute to a collaborative workflow, ensuring smooth coordination and alignment. Growth & Innovation Stay updated on design trends, tools, and best practices Contribute ideas to enhance visual storytelling and communication approaches Leverage new technologies and tools to propose innovative solutions. What You’ll Bring:   ≥2 years of professional experience in visual design, preferably in a creative agency or design studio Bachelor’s degree preferred in graphic design, visual arts, or a related field Proficiency in design software such as Adobe Creative Suite (Photoshop, Illustrator, InDesign) and Microsoft Office Suite (PowerPoint, Word) Solid understanding of typography, color theory, and design principles Excellent communication skills and ability to collaborate effectively with cross-functional teams Attention to detail and a passion for delivering high-quality work Curiosity about Generative AI and emerging design tech. Good to Have Basic skills in motion graphics/video editing (After Effects, Premiere Pro) Familiarity with web/UI tools (Figma, Adobe XD, Sketch, Framer) Experience with Google Slides, Sites, and other G Suite tools. Portfolio Requirement [ Must Included in Resume] Strong portfolio (at least 3–5 pieces) demonstrating conceptual thinking and execution across various mediums. What Success Looks Like Ability to take design projects from brief to final delivery with minimal supervision Deliver clean, well-structured handoffs and maintain efficient workflows Collaborate effectively as a proactive and dependable team player Maintain structured project management, including proper file and folder naming and organization. What You'll Bring Fluency in English Client-first mentality Intense work ethic Collaborative spirit and problem-solving approac

description: description
responsibilities: Media and Analyst Relations Associate Consultant Imagine shaping how a global consulting and tech firm is seen across India. Turning stories into headlines, building connections with media, and making our leaders the go-to voices in tech. Join our Marketing & Communications team in Gurugram as we strengthen our position as a global tech partner and employer of choice in the India region. As a Software Engineer - Big Data/Java/Scala at JPMorgan Chase, you will join the Liquidity risk (LRI) team to design and implement the next generation buildout of a cloud native liquidity risk (LRI) management platform for JPMC.  The Liquidity risk technology organization aims to provide comprehensive solution to managing the firm’s liquidity risk and to meet our regulatory reporting obligations across 50+ markets.   The program will include strategic the buildout of advanced liquidity calculation engines, incorporate AI and ML into our liquidity risk processes, and bring digital first reporting capabilities. The target platform must process 40-60 million transactions and positions daily, calculate risk presented by the current actual as well as model-based what-if state of the market, build multidimensional picture of corporate risk profile, and provide ability to analyze it in real time
